channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
item.description: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.
item.content:encoded:
Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

(Image credit: Michal Cizek)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
item.description: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.

(Image credit: Danielle Villasana)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran's strike wounded over a dozen U.S. personnel and hit valuable jets in Saudi Arabia
item.description: NPR has confirmed that at least two U.S. E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and more than a dozen U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Saudi Arabia on Friday.
item.content:encoded:
NPR has confirmed that at least two U.S. E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and more than a dozen U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Saudi Arabia on Friday.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: China's AI chatbots are advanced and versatile — and begging for more users
item.description: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

(Image credit: ADEK BERRY/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: A skeleton discovered in a Dutch church may belong to musketeer d'Artagnan
item.description: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.
item.content:encoded:
Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.

(Image credit: Arterra)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
item.description: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Central Haitian town descends into fire and bloodshed from gang warfare
item.description: Violence erupted in the central Haitian town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite early Sunday morning as a powerful gang warred with a vigilante group.
item.content:encoded:
Violence erupted in the central Haitian town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite early Sunday morning as a powerful gang warred with a vigilante group.

(Image credit: Odelyn Joseph)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
item.description: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.
item.content:encoded: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims that God justifies war in Palm Sunday Mass message
item.description: Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.

(Image credit: Remo Casilli)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks
item.description: A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.
item.content:encoded:
A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
